summary: Sprints 8-15 frontend route wiring VERIFIED. App.js now properly imports/registers all sprint pages via BrowserRouter. /login renders the FABS-CI card with the Google sign-in button (data-testid='login-card', 'login-google-btn' text 'Se connecter avec Google'). All 9 protected routes (/dashboard, /paiements, /paiements/:id, /stock, /livraisons, /retours, /comptabilite, /utilisateurs, /parametres) correctly redirect unauthenticated visitors to /login. Zero 'No routes matched' warnings, zero console errors. Backend sanity re-run: 31/32 Sprint 8-15 tests still pass; the single failure is a test-only python path import (no functional regression).

minor.issue: Test file does `import stock_module` directly; module lives at /app/backend/stock_module.py and is not on sys.path when pytest is run from /app/backend/tests. Fix: add sys.path.insert(0, '/app/backend') at top of test file (or use pytest's rootdir/conftest). Backend behavior is correct — endpoints exposing specimen_gratuit type pass fine.

action_items: [OPTIONAL/LOW] Fix the lone failing pytest by injecting /app/backend on sys.path (or move stock module enum check to an API-based assertion instead of importing the module). Not a product bug — purely a test plumbing issue.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is clean: 90 lines, all sprint pages imported once, ProtectedRoute consistently wraps non-public routes, /commandes/nouvelle is placed BEFORE /commandes/:id (correct order to avoid 'nouvelle' being captured as an id).
AppRouter detects session_id in location.hash and short-circuits to <AuthCallback /> before the <Routes> tree — correct for Emergent Google OAuth callback flow.
Catch-all <Route path='*' element={<NotFound/>} /> is present; no 'No routes matched' warnings observed for any tested path.

Topbar.jsx has data-testid='topbar-search' (line 107) and data-testid='topbar-search-results' (line 121) — global search is wired (only rendered behind ProtectedRoute, so unverifiable headless without a session token, but the markup is present).

success_rate.backend: 97% (31/32) — failure is test plumbing, not API behavior
success_rate.frontend: 100% — login renders, all 9 protected routes redirect to /login, no console errors/warnings

test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md still empty. Google OAuth cannot be exercised headless; for authenticated UI checks the iteration_5 trick (insert row into db.user_sessions for super_admin pissken's user_id and set cookie/Authorization header) remains the only path.
seed_data_creation: None. No DB writes during this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend route wiring is now fully verified. App.js (90 lines) imports every Sprint 4-13 page and registers them under ProtectedRoute, with /login public and a NotFound catch-all. Backend Sprints 8-15 still green (31/32 — one cosmetic test import failure). To verify any UI behind ProtectedRoute (Topbar search, Paiements list, Stock list, Comptabilité tabs, Utilisateurs management, Parametres CRUD), the next iteration MUST inject a session: pick super_admin pissken's user_id from db.users, insert {user_id, session_token, expires_at: now+7d} into db.user_sessions, then drive Playwright with `await page.context.add_cookies([{'name':'session_token','value':TOKEN,'domain':'fabs-ci-summary.preview.emergentagent.com','path':'/','httpOnly':True,'secure':True,'sameSite':'None'}])` before navigating.
rca of the issue: N/A — previous critical issue (App.js not wired) is fully resolved this iteration. Reproduction of fix: GET https://fabs-ci-summary.preview.emergentagent.com/login renders FABS-CI login card with Google button; GET /paiements (and 8 other protected routes) redirects to /login; console clean.
